page: Amazon Web Services c8i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor architecture includes 480 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for vector and matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier capabilities in multi-threaded and memory-intensive operations. The instance achieved a PassMark CPU score of 106,933.38 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 22,352.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory read bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 7,683.33 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 388.68 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 reached 662.56 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations registered at 171.2 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference processed 320.04 tokens per second for 7B model prompts and 41.44 tokens per second for 70B model prompts.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.24xlarge offers high compute density and memory bandwidth relative to its resource profile, making it efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy applications. Additionally, the memory allocation is limited to 2.0 GB per core, which may constrain memory-bound tasks. This instance is best suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture with the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory, 480 MB of L3 cache, and a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier performance, including a PassMark CPU score of 106,933.38 and 171.2 million Redis SET operations per second. Lacking local storage and GPUs, the instance presents a tradeoff for storage-heavy or graphics-accelerated tasks but delivers high compute density. It is suited for software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring advanced Intel Xeon processors, rapid DDR5 memory bandwidth, and optimized vector execution capabilities.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor
192.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector calculations
Top-tier multi-core performance, including high-throughput Redis and database operations
No local storage or GPU hardware, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Best suited for software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
